description: Manifest V3 Chrome extension that replicates OpenClaw browser automation as a standalone plugin - agentic tool-use loop with accessibility tree snapshots, CDP-based actions, and OpenAI-compatible LLM integration

userStories.title: Project scaffolding
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project initialized with build tooling so that TypeScript source compiles into a loadable Chrome extension.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json with name browser-use-extension, scripts: dev, build, typecheck
Dependencies: vite, @crxjs/vite-plugin, typescript
tsconfig.json targeting ES2022, strict mode, module ESNext, moduleResolution bundler
vite.config.ts using @crxjs/vite-plugin with manifest import

manifest.json at project root with manifest_version 3, permissions: [debugger, activeTab, tabs, sidePanel, storage], host_permissions: [https://*/*, http://*/*], background.service_worker, side_panel.default_path, options_page, action with icons
Placeholder src/background/service-worker.ts (empty export), src/sidepanel/index.html (minimal HTML), src/options/index.html (minimal HTML)
Placeholder icon PNGs at public/icons/16.png, public/icons/48.png, public/icons/128.png (simple colored squares)
.gitignore includes node_modules/ and dist/
npm run build succeeds and produces dist/ with valid extension files
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Chrome 118+ minimum. Use @crxjs/vite-plugin@beta if needed for Vite 5+ compatibility.

userStories.title: Shared type definitions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared TypeScript types so all modules use consistent interfaces.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/background/types.ts
RefEntry interface: backendNodeId: number, nodeId?: number, role: string, name: string, value?: string, properties?: Record<string, unknown>
RefMap type as Map<string, RefEntry>
HybridSnapshot interface: text: string, screenshotBase64: string, refMap: RefMap, metadata: { url, title, truncated, totalElements, visibleElements }
ActionResult interface: success: boolean, data?: unknown, error?: string
LLMConfig interface: apiBaseUrl, apiKey, modelName, maxTokens, temperature, screenshotDetail ('low'|'high'), visionEnabled boolean
AgentState interface: status ('idle'|'running'|'paused'|'error'), conversationHistory: ChatMessage[], currentTabId: number|null, iteration: number, maxIterations: number, actionDelayMs: number
ChatMessage type matching OpenAI chat completion format (text content, image_url content blocks, tool_calls, tool role messages)
PanelMessage union type for all side panel <-> service worker message types: user_prompt, stop, status, action, action_result, agent_message, error, snapshot_preview
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CDP Manager
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a wrapper around chrome.debugger that provides a clean async interface for sending CDP commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/background/cdp-manager.ts exporting CDPManager class
attach(tabId): Promise<void> — wraps chrome.debugger.attach(target, '1.3') in Promise, tracks in Set<number>, enables Accessibility.enable + Page.enable + Network.enable after attach
detach(tabId): Promise<void> — wraps chrome.debugger.detach, removes from tracking set
detachAll(): Promise<void> — detaches all tracked tabs
isAttached(tabId): boolean — checks tracking set
send<T>(tabId, method, params?): Promise<T> — wraps chrome.debugger.sendCommand in Promise
Prevents double-attach (no-op if already attached)
Listens to chrome.debugger.onDetach to clean up tracking set
Listens to chrome.debugger.onEvent, exposes via onEvent(callback) method
Exposes onDetach(callback) for external listeners
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Accessibility CDP domain is experimental but stable in practice. Handle unexpected response shapes gracefully.

userStories.title: Snapshot engine — accessibility tree and ref assignment
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to capture the accessibility tree from a page and assign numbered refs to interactive/named elements.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/background/snapshot-engine.ts exporting SnapshotEngine class that takes CDPManager
takeSnapshot(tabId): Promise<HybridSnapshot> is the main method
Calls Accessibility.getFullAXTree via CDP Manager
Walks tree and assigns refs (e1, e2, e3...) based on role categories
Interactive roles (button, checkbox, combobox, link, menuitem, menuitemcheckbox, menuitemradio, option, radio, searchbox, slider, spinbutton, switch, tab, textbox, treeitem) always get a ref

Content roles (heading, img, listitem, cell, row, table, list, paragraph, blockquote, code, contentinfo, navigation, banner, main, complementary, form, region, alert, dialog, status, tooltip) get a ref only when they have a name
All other roles (generic, group, div, etc.) are skipped
Builds RefMap mapping ref strings to RefEntry objects with backendNodeId, role, name, value, properties
Formats text with 2-space indentation per level, e.g. [e1] button 'Submit'
Header line: Page: 'title' | URL: url
Shows values for input elements (textbox, combobox, etc.)
Annotates focused/checked/disabled states
Configurable maxDepth (default 10) and maxChars (default 30000)
Appends '[truncated - N more elements not shown]' when truncated
Calls Page.captureScreenshot for viewport screenshot as base64 PNG
Returns complete HybridSnapshot with text, screenshotBase64, refMap, metadata
RefMap rebuilt on every call (refs are ephemeral)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a larger story but the logic is tightly coupled — tree walking, ref assignment, and formatting must be done together.

userStories.title: Action executor — base and ref resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a base action executor that can resolve refs to DOM coordinates so individual actions can target elements.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/background/action-executor.ts exporting ActionExecutor class that takes CDPManager
Stores RefMap reference, updated via setRefMap(refMap) method
resolveRef(ref) — looks up ref in RefMap, throws descriptive error if not found ('ref e5 not found — element may have changed, take a new snapshot')
getElementCenter(tabId, backendNodeId): Promise<{x, y}> — calls DOM.getBoxModel, extracts center from content quad: x=(quad[0]+quad[2]+quad[4]+quad[6])/4, y=(quad[1]+quad[3]+quad[5]+quad[7])/4
resolveObjectId(tabId, backendNodeId): Promise<string> — calls DOM.resolveNode, returns objectId
execute(tabId, toolName, args): Promise<ActionResult> — dispatcher that routes to action methods, wraps in try/catch returning ActionResult
All errors return ActionResult with success: false and descriptive error string
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DOM.getBoxModel returns {model: {content, padding, border, margin, width, height}} where content is a Quad: [x1,y1, x2,y2, x3,y3, x4,y4].

userStories.title: Action executor — click
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the click action so the agent can click on page elements.
acceptanceCriteria: Add click(tabId, ref, options?) method to ActionExecutor
Calls DOM.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded({backendNodeId}) before clicking
Gets element center via getElementCenter
Dispatches three mouse events via Input.dispatchMouseEvent: mouseMoved, mousePressed (button: left, clickCount: 1), mouseReleased
Supports doubleClick option (clickCount: 2)
Supports button option (left/right/middle)
Supports modifiers option (Alt=1, Control=2, Meta=4, Shift=8 bitmask)
Returns ActionResult with success: true
Registered in execute dispatcher
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Action executor — type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the type action so the agent can enter text into input fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Add type(tabId, ref, text, options?) method to ActionExecutor
Calls DOM.focus({backendNodeId}) to focus the element
Clears existing value via Runtime.callFunctionOn with function: function(){this.value='';this.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true}))}
Types text via Input.insertText({text})
Supports submit option: dispatches keyDown + keyUp for Enter key after typing
Supports slowly option: dispatches individual keyDown/keyUp per character instead of insertText
Returns ActionResult with success: true
Registered in execute dispatcher
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Action executor — navigate
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the navigate action so the agent can go to URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add navigate(tabId, url) method to ActionExecutor
Calls Page.navigate({url}) via CDP
Waits for page load by listening for Page.loadEventFired or polling document.readyState via Runtime.evaluate
Returns ActionResult with success: true and data: { url }
Handles navigation errors (invalid URL, network error) with descriptive error messages
Registered in execute dispatcher
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Action executor — screenshot
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the screenshot action so the agent can capture page visuals on demand.
acceptanceCriteria: Add screenshot(tabId, options?) method to ActionExecutor
Calls Page.captureScreenshot({format: 'png'}) via CDP
Supports fullPage option using captureBeyondViewport: true
Returns ActionResult with success: true and data: { base64: string }
Registered in execute dispatcher
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Action executor — press, scroll, hover
userStories.description: As a developer, I need keyboard and pointer actions for navigation and interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Add press(tabId, key) method — dispatches keyDown + keyUp via Input.dispatchKeyEvent with proper key and code values; supports Enter, Tab, Escape, Arrow keys, Backspace, Delete, and standard characters
Add scroll(tabId, ref) method — calls DOM.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded({backendNodeId})
Add hover(tabId, ref) method — gets element center via getElementCenter, dispatches mouseMoved event
All three return ActionResult with success: true
All three registered in execute dispatcher
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Action executor — drag
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the drag action for drag-and-drop interactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add drag(tabId, startRef, endRef) method to ActionExecutor
Gets center coordinates for both start and end elements
Dispatches mousePressed at start position (button: left)
Interpolates movement in ~10 steps from start to end, dispatching mouseMoved at each step
Dispatches mouseReleased at end position
Returns ActionResult with success: true
Registered in execute dispatcher
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Action executor — select
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the select action for dropdown/select elements.
acceptanceCriteria: Add select(tabId, ref, values) method to ActionExecutor
Resolves element objectId via DOM.resolveNode
Calls Runtime.callFunctionOn with helper that iterates <option> children, sets selected on matching options by value, dispatches change and input events with bubbles: true
Returns ActionResult with success: true and data: { selected: string[] }
Registered in execute dispatcher
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Action executor — fill (batch form fill)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the fill action to batch-fill multiple form fields in one tool call.
acceptanceCriteria: Add fill(tabId, fields) method where fields is Array<{ref, type, value}>
For checkbox/radio type: checks current state via CDP, clicks if state doesn't match desired value
For select type: calls this.select(tabId, ref, [value])
For text/other types: calls this.type(tabId, ref, value)
Returns ActionResult with success: true and data: { filled: number }
Registered in execute dispatcher
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Action executor — wait
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the wait action so the agent can pause until page conditions are met.
acceptanceCriteria: Add wait(tabId, conditions) method to ActionExecutor
Supports text condition: polls Runtime.evaluate checking document.body.innerText.includes(text)
Supports textGone condition: polls checking !document.body.innerText.includes(text)
Supports selector condition: polls checking document.querySelector(selector) !== null
Supports url condition: polls checking location.href matches pattern (simple glob with *)
Supports fn condition: polls evaluating custom JS expression for truthy return
Polls every 200ms
Configurable timeoutMs (default 10000)
Returns ActionResult with success: true when condition met, success: false with error on timeout
Registered in execute dispatcher
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Action executor — evaluate
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the evaluate action so the agent can run JavaScript in the page context.
acceptanceCriteria: Add evaluate(tabId, fn, ref?) method to ActionExecutor
If ref provided: resolves objectId, calls Runtime.callFunctionOn({objectId, functionDeclaration: fn, returnByValue: true})
If no ref: calls Runtime.evaluate({expression: fn, returnByValue: true})
Returns ActionResult with success: true and data set to evaluated result
Handles evaluation errors (syntax, runtime exceptions) with descriptive messages
Registered in execute dispatcher
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Action executor — tab operations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tab management actions so the agent can work across multiple tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tabList() method — calls chrome.tabs.query({}), returns ActionResult with data: Array<{id, title, url, active}>
Add tabOpen(url) method — calls chrome.tabs.create({url}), returns new tab info
Add tabClose(tabId) method — calls chrome.tabs.remove(tabId), detaches debugger if attached
Add tabFocus(tabId) method — calls chrome.tabs.update(tabId, {active: true}), attaches debugger if not already attached
All four registered in execute dispatcher as tab_list, tab_open, tab_close, tab_focus
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Action executor — cookies
userStories.description: As a developer, I need cookie management actions for the agent.
acceptanceCriteria: Add cookiesGet(tabId) method — calls Network.getCookies({urls: [currentUrl]}) via CDP, returns cookie array
Add cookiesSet(tabId, params) method — calls Network.setCookie({name, value, url, domain?, path?, secure?, httpOnly?}) via CDP
Add cookiesClear(tabId) method — gets current URL, calls Network.deleteCookies for all cookies on current domain
All three registered in execute dispatcher as cookies_get, cookies_set, cookies_clear
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Action executor — set viewport and PDF export
userStories.description: As a developer, I need viewport emulation and PDF export actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add setViewport(tabId, width, height) method — calls Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride({width, height, deviceScaleFactor: 1, mobile: false})
Add pdf(tabId) method — calls Page.printToPDF({}) via CDP, returns ActionResult with data: { base64: string }
Both registered in execute dispatcher as set_viewport and pdf
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tool definitions for LLM
userStories.description: As a developer, I need OpenAI function-calling format tool schemas to send to the LLM.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/background/tool-definitions.ts exporting const toolDefinitions array

Contains all 22 tool schemas: snapshot, click, type, navigate, screenshot, press, scroll, hover, drag, select, fill, wait, evaluate, tab_list, tab_open, tab_close, tab_focus, cookies_get, cookies_set, cookies_clear, set_viewport, pdf
Each tool has type: 'function', function: {name, description, parameters} matching OpenAI format
Parameter types, enums, and required arrays match the design spec exactly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Refer to docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-18-browser-use-extension-kernel-design.md section 6 for exact tool schemas.

userStories.title: LLM client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an API client that calls any OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint with tool definitions and vision support.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/background/llm-client.ts exporting chatCompletion function
Signature: chatCompletion(messages, tools, config: LLMConfig): Promise<ChatCompletionResponse>
POST to ${config.apiBaseUrl}/chat/completions with model, messages, tools, tool_choice: 'auto', max_tokens, temperature
Headers: Content-Type application/json, Authorization Bearer ${config.apiKey}
If config.visionEnabled is false, strips image_url content blocks from messages before sending
Retries on 429 and 5xx with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s), max 3 retries
On 401/403 returns clear error immediately (no retry)
Parses and returns typed response with message.content and message.tool_calls
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Agent loop — core orchestration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core agent loop that ties snapshot, LLM, and actions together into an autonomous cycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/background/agent-loop.ts exporting AgentLoop class
Constructor takes CDPManager, SnapshotEngine, ActionExecutor, and LLMConfig
start(userPrompt, tabId): Promise<void> initiates the loop
On start: sets status running, attaches debugger, takes initial hybrid snapshot, builds conversation history with system prompt + observation + user prompt
System prompt matches spec template (describes refs, screenshot, tools, instructions)
Observation messages use OpenAI multi-part content format: text block (snapshot) + image_url block (base64 PNG with configurable detail)
Loop calls LLM, if text response with no tool_calls: emits agent_message, sets idle, breaks
If tool_calls: executes each via ActionExecutor, collects tool result messages, takes fresh snapshot if page changed
Page change detection: compares URL/title before and after, or if action was navigate/click/tab_focus/tab_open
Respects maxIterations (default 50), emits error if exceeded
Respects actionDelayMs (default 500ms) between actions
stop() method sets status idle, checked before each LLM call and action
Emits callbacks: onStatus, onAction, onActionResult, onMessage, onError, onSnapshotPreview
Persists conversation history to chrome.storage.session on stop for service worker survival
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the core engine. Large but tightly coupled — splitting would create artificial boundaries.

userStories.title: Service worker — entry point and message routing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the service worker to initialize all modules and route messages between the side panel and agent loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/background/service-worker.ts as main entry point
Instantiates CDPManager, SnapshotEngine, ActionExecutor, AgentLoop
Loads LLMConfig from chrome.storage.local on startup
Listens to chrome.runtime.onConnect for 'agent-panel' port connections
On user_prompt message: loads latest config, calls agentLoop.start(prompt, activeTabId)
On stop message: calls agentLoop.stop()
Wires AgentLoop callbacks to forward messages to panel port (status, action, action_result, agent_message, error, snapshot_preview)
Handles port.onDisconnect: stops agent loop, cleans up
Sets chrome.sidePanel.setPanelBehavior({ openPanelOnActionClick: true })
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Side panel — HTML and CSS
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the side panel markup and styles for the chat interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/sidepanel/index.html with: message log container (scrollable div), text input + send button, status bar, stop button (hidden when idle), settings gear button
Create src/sidepanel/panel.css with minimal functional styling
Message log fills available height, scrolls vertically
User messages right-aligned with distinct background color
Agent actions in monospace font
Agent responses left-aligned
Error messages in red
Status bar at bottom showing idle/thinking/acting/error
Stop button prominent (red/warning color), hidden when idle
Input stretches to fill width, send button beside it
Clean readable typography with system font stack
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Side panel — chat logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the side panel TypeScript that connects to the service worker and drives the chat UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/sidepanel/panel.ts loaded by index.html
On load connects to service worker: chrome.runtime.connect({ name: 'agent-panel' })
Send button sends {type: 'user_prompt', text} via port, appends user message to log, clears input, disables input + send
Enter key in input triggers send
Stop button sends {type: 'stop'} via port

Port message handler renders by type: status updates bar and toggles stop/input; action appends monospace tool+args; action_result shows success/failure; agent_message appends response; error appends in red; snapshot_preview shows in collapsible
Message log auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages
Settings gear opens options via chrome.runtime.openOptionsPage()
Handles port.onDisconnect gracefully (shows reconnect message)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Options page — HTML, CSS, and logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the options page where users configure their LLM API connection.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/options/index.html with form: API Base URL (text, placeholder https://api.openai.com/v1), API Key (password, with 'stored unencrypted' note), Model Name (text, placeholder gpt-4o), Max Tokens (number, default 4096), Temperature (number step 0.1, default 0.2), Screenshot Detail (select low|high), Vision Enabled (checkbox, default checked), Max Iterations (number, default 50), Action Delay (number ms, default 500), Save button, status message area
Create src/options/options.css with clean form styling (labels above inputs, max-width, readable spacing)
Create src/options/options.ts: on load reads config from chrome.storage.local and populates form
Save button writes all fields to chrome.storage.local, shows brief 'Saved' confirmation
Validates API Base URL starts with http:// or https://
Validates numeric fields (max tokens > 0, temperature 0-2, iterations > 0, delay >= 0)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integration — end-to-end wiring and build verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all modules wired together so the extension builds and loads as a complete unit.
acceptanceCriteria: Service worker imports and initializes all modules in correct dependency order
Vite config properly bundles all entry points: service-worker.ts, sidepanel/index.html, options/index.html
npm run build produces a complete loadable extension in dist/
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
No circular dependency issues between modules
manifest.json references correct paths (handled by @crxjs/vite-plugin)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story is about fixing any integration issues that emerge when all pieces come together. May involve adjusting imports, fixing type mismatches, or tweaking vite config.
